notes: 38 insulation LWPOLYLINEs (5-vtx closed cells) on plan $INSUL layer; wall-cavity fill fragment, no wall face-lines present in the block.

rationale.evidence: All 38 entities are LWPOLYLINE on layer X-평면도(기본형)$0$INSUL (insulation), each a closed 5-vertex quad (e.g. handles 44F5, 44F6); the dxf histogram is 100% LWPOLYLINE with no LINE entities, so bbox is not derivable. Content is homogeneous insulation fill with no wall-outline, text, or dimension geometry.
rationale.rule: A definition whose entities are exclusively closed 5-vertex polylines on a plan-view '...$INSUL' layer is wall-cavity insulation hatch — a plan-construction fragment, not a reusable icon, a dimension cache, or furniture. Insulation only occurs inside walls, so the block is wall-associated even though it carries no wall face-lines itself.
rationale.counterfactual: If these polylines sat on a WALL/벽 layer, or were long 2-point segments instead of small closed quads, they would read as wall lines and push wall_likelihood toward 1; a fixed identical entity_count reused across blocks would instead argue 심볼, and added door/text/furniture geometry would argue a richer 평면 부분도 or 가구.
rationale.uncertainty: Vertex coordinates and bbox are not exposed, so I cannot confirm the cells trace a linear wall run, measure wall thickness, or separate exterior from party walls; whether a distinct wall-outline layer accompanies this insulation elsewhere is undeterminable from this projection.
